programmes.show_description: Jeremy Clarkson hosts a quiz in which six hopefuls compete for £1 million, with only one player at a time able to take a place in the Hot Seat and answer multiple-choice questions to work the way up the Money Ladder. Each question is against the clock and if a player gets one wrong they are eliminated, the Top Prize drops down, and the next player takes their place

Graham Norton hosts the game show as firefighter Kerrie, financial adviser Richard and account manager Ciaran compete to solve word puzzles to get the chance to spin the wheel and win big money prizes, with a potential jackpot of £50,000
Daily escape designed to calm the mind and encourage relaxation and reflection

The Fletchers prepare for their biggest event of the year in the form of a six-day-long Christmas Celebrations gala that welcomes families to the farm for festive fun. Elsewhere, the family's much-loved pet Ginger gives birth to her puppies, and the flock gets lost in the fog
Morning magazine featuring a lively mix of news and current affairs, plus health, entertainment and lifestyle features
A mix of celebrity chat, lifestyle features, advice and competitions
The key headlines from the day so far

Stephen Mulhern hosts the game show, with Denise Lewis, Deborah Meaden and Kevin Whately all trying to say what they see, as they guess well-known phrases for the chance to win a £50,000 jackpot for their chosen good causes

The fast-paced game of words, hosted by Adil Ray, where three pairs of contestants face Lingo grids, fiendish puzzlewords, and a tense head-to-head round as they battle to reach the final and a chance to win a 15,000-pound jackpot. Crossing words alongside the reigning champions are Birmingham couple Thofeq and Muslima and auntie and nephew Jemma and Jonathan

Ranvir Singh hosts the quiz as Jonathan and Dale, Shivani and Lokesh, and Lily and Torie take on Riddlemaster Henry Lewis as they attempt to answer general knowledge questions and confusing conundrums. Only one team will make it through to the high-stakes final with a chance to win a cash prize

Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

Bradley Walsh hosts as Leicester native Jo, Andrew from London, Irene from Doncaster and Charlie from Manchester work as a team to take on one of the experts in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught
A round-up of the day's news headlines and talking points from the UK and around the world

Kelvin and Liz scan their sheep to find out how many are pregnant, while Marnie and Milo head out on an egg hunt when their chickens become very free range and decide to lay all over the farm, a field undergoes repairs as rotten posts get pulled out ready for an extension and the family prepare to welcome cows to the farm
Bear makes a huge revelation, while Graham works hard to keep Joe on side and Jacob is struggling at work
Sam falls unconscious in the street, Kit encounters a witness to his assault on Mal, and Christina orders the undertakers to cheer up

Annie and her colleagues begin to see things in a new light as fresh information continues to emerge. Two suspects' fates hang in the balance as they try to account for their movements on the days surrounding Sarah's disappearance, and insights into their characters paint a muddy picture. As Michael's relationship with his daughter Alana comes under strain, the weight of suspicion tips towards one suspect before an encounter pushes Annie's professional and private lives together
A fresh perspective on the major stories of the day, featuring national and international news, with reports and analysis from ITV's correspondents
Current affairs show tackling the issues behind the headlines from a Scottish perspective, with studio debate and analysis of major stories

programmes.show_name: Olivia Attwood: The Price of Perfection
programmes.show_description: Olivia explores the UK's weight-management industry, which is worth more than two billion pounds, but the trends in how people go about losing weight are changing. She heads to Turkey to watch one of the most extreme liposuction surgeries on the market and chats to former model Lottie Moss, who had a near-death experience using Ozempic

programmes.show_name: Martin Clunes' Islands of the Atlantic
programmes.show_description: The actor embarks on an epic journey to explore the ocean's islands, beginning with tropical jungles and grassland savannah off the western coast of Africa. He encounters giant snails and helps rescue endangered sea turtles in equatorial Sao Tome and Principe, which is also known as the African Galapagos. He then treks through savannah on the remote Bijagos Islands off Guinea Bissau to meet ferocious predators and a tribe in which the women hold the power
News, sport and weather from across Scotland

Camilla Bassett-Smith explores a sub-tropical garden in Cornwall and Claire Lowrie shares her favourite houseplant tools. Henry Agg demonstrates how to create a DIY garden path and Poppy Okotcha invites Alan into her Devon garden for a sustainable gardening masterclass

programmes.show_name: The Martin Lewis Money Show: Live
programmes.show_description: The cash expert is back with his Big Briefing, breaking down the week's biggest financial headlines and answering viewers' questions live. Plus, News You Can Use is packed with the not-to-be-missed deals of the week
Paddy and Dylan make a big confession, while the Dingles are suspicious of Joe Tate and Pollard plays detective
Leanne persuades Sam to share his troubles, Kit is forced to question his own victim, Alya fails to match Adam's enthusiasm, and Todd spots Theo lunching with another man

The final four make the last terrifying ascent, with obstacles in the way including a dangerous crevasse crossing that sees one of them frozen in fear and another's attempt made harder by the menacing Mountain's Keeper still lurking around, Who will make it to the Summit and claim their share of the cash prize?

The best moments from the 98th Academy Awards ceremony at the Dolby Theatre in Hollywood, which was hosted by Conan O'Brien, and saw Jessie Buckley in the running for an Oscar after her turn in Hamnet
